title: Scrum/Standup/Daily Update
summary: The meeting focused on resolving voicemail drop issues with modern phones, leading to an alert system strategy. Dashboard security and performance for the Lovable application were reviewed, alongside updates on chat widgets and AI analysis for VCONs. Data collection for problem magnitude was prioritized.

decisions.description: Implement an alert system to flag when a human answers during a voicemail drop for callback.
Cut off previous call audio when initiating a new call to prevent agent confusion.
Prioritize data collection for one week to gauge voicemail drop problem magnitude.
Publish Lovable dashboard publicly with Google authentication, share link internally.
Re-run security scan for Lovable dashboard before wider deployment.

actionItems.task: Keep discussion about unintended voicemail drop with Shauna on notes for tomorrow.
Push an update related to tightening up security for Starling GPT.
Cut off the audio from the previous call as soon as another call comes in.
Set up a new Slack channel for alerts regarding customers picking up during a voicemail drop.
Create a card for the new Slack channel and include the list of people to add.
Go over the cards in the BDC performance dashboard with Derek and Anthony for feedback.
Look into and rerun the security scan for the BDC performance dashboard.
Compare the Lovable AI analysis of customer comments with the Snowflake AI assistant.
Take a look at the Metabase question that Kevin Breen sent in Slack.
Make configuration update for Hopkins Ford chat button text to 'chat'.

tensions: Agent confusion and chaos due to simultaneous audio from multiple calls.
Technical complexity and potential slowness of a 'jump back into call' button.
Concern about investing development time into a problem of unknown frequency.
Potential database performance bottlenecks for the Lovable dashboard with high usage.
Concerns about Snowflake costs if dashboards become very popular and frequently refreshed.
Outdated security audit for the Lovable dashboard prior to Google authentication.
Lovable AI's subpar performance in analyzing VCON customer comments, missing context.
Suspicion of templated or fake negative customer comments from some providers.
referencesToPast: Anticipated voicemail drop issue: 'we knew this was going to come up'.
Previous discussions about Sophia handling customer jumps during voicemail.
Security audit issue predating Google authentication for Lovable dashboard.
Kevin Breen's prior meeting with Anthony and Derek regarding dealer reports.

contributions.description: Proposed an alert system for human answers during voicemail drops for callback.
Championed the alert system for human answers during voicemail drops for callback.
Reported agent confusion and chaos from hearing multiple audio streams simultaneously.
Committed to fixing the audio issue by cutting off previous call audio on new call initiation.
Suggested running LLM analysis on past voicemail transcripts to estimate problem frequency.
Raised concern about spending excessive development time on a problem of unknown frequency.
Proposed using OpenAI real-time API for partially interactive voicemail drops to handle answers.
Successfully integrated Google login into the Lovable dashboard for restricted access.
Expressed concern about potential database performance bottlenecks for the Lovable dashboard.
Suggested initially publishing the Lovable dashboard for internal workspace only.
Suggested publishing the Lovable dashboard publicly with Google auth, sharing link internally.
Confirmed that the Lovable dashboard routes are protected after a quick check.
Reported subpar performance of Lovable's AI in analyzing VCON customer comments.
Identified identical negative customer comments across VCONs, suggesting templated or fake data.
Suggested using call transcripts/summaries instead of agent-inputted lead record comments for VCON analysis.
